title: JARVIS Step 38 — Read-Only Ask Answer-Quality Improvement
purpose: Improve /api/jarvis/ask answers for common operator questions so JARVIS gives useful read-only status summaries (especially the trading posture) instead of generic safety text, while preserving every safety and refusal behavior. The handler still executes nothing: no commands, no trades, no refresh, no writes, no storage. Trading answers are derived only from the existing read-only _jarvis_trading_detail file scan (read_only / paper_ready / live_ready / broker_control).
problem_observed: 'how are we doing with trading?' previously fell through to UNSUPPORTED (refused) OR, for the posture phrasing, returned a generic read-only shell explanation. It should instead summarize the actual read-only trading posture from existing JARVIS status context.
files_changed: jarvis_conversation_safety.py — extended _SAFE_INFO with read-only trading-status patterns (how are we doing / ready for paper|live trading / paper_ready|live_ready / broker_control / trading status) so those questions classify SAFE_INFO instead of fail-closing to UNSUPPORTED. Forbidden patterns are unchanged and still checked FIRST, so no action-request can leak through.

app.py — added _jarvis_trading_posture_answer(), a read-only helper that reads the four posture fields from _jarvis_safe(_jarvis_trading_detail) and builds a direct posture summary (conservative per-field fallback 'I cannot verify that field from the current read-only status.' when a field is not a bool). Routed trading-status questions in the SAFE_INFO branch of _jarvis_ask_answer to this helper, replacing the old hardcoded 'posture' string.

tests/test_jarvis_ask_contract.py — added a Step 38 section (trading questions safe + report posture fields; no invented performance claims; forbidden questions still refused; mixed-intent forbidden override; status shape unchanged after a trading ask).
tests/test_jarvis_conversation_safety.py — added Step 38 classifier tests (trading-status questions -> SAFE_INFO; trading-action questions still FORBIDDEN).
explicitly_not_done: No templates/jarvis.html change (UI untouched; still sends only {question}).
No refresh / execution / broker / paper / live control.
No chat / audio / transcript storage; no localStorage / sessionStorage / IndexedDB / cookies.
No trade run or simulated; no data fetched.
No broker / live / paper trading logic touched.
No trading / Factory / S26-S28 / Donchian / Hydra / base.html / storage file touched.
No change to the /api/jarvis/ask payload shape — still {question} only.
No change to /api/jarvis/status shape — still 24 top-level keys.
No invented performance / profit / readiness / validation claims.

answer_quality_summary.trading_posture_answer: Trading is observation-only. read_only=true. paper_ready=false. live_ready=false. broker_control=false. This means JARVIS is not authorized to trade or control broker/paper/live systems.
answer_quality_summary.source_of_truth: _jarvis_safe(_jarvis_trading_detail) — read-only file scan; never runs a backtest, fetches data, calls a broker, or writes.
answer_quality_summary.missing_field_behavior: Any field not readable as a bool is reported as 'I cannot verify that field from the current read-only status.' (no guessing).
answer_quality_summary.style: Direct, useful, conservative; no hype, no trading advice, no permission language.
answer_quality_summary.other_improved_answers_unchanged: why is commander yellow? / what needs attention? / what does read_only mean? / what JARVIS docs exist? continue to answer as before (SAFE_INFO / SAFE_EXPLAIN).

before_after_how_are_we_doing_with_tradi.question: how are we doing with trading?
before_after_how_are_we_doing_with_tradi.before: UNSUPPORTED / generic read-only shell: 'This is answerable from the read-only JARVIS status aggregate; it reports observed state only and authorizes no action.' (or refused fail-closed)
before_after_how_are_we_doing_with_tradi.after: SAFE_INFO, refused=false: 'Trading is observation-only. read_only=true. paper_ready=false. live_ready=false. broker_control=false. This means JARVIS is not authorized to trade or control broker/paper/live systems.' sources_used=['trading_detail']

forbidden_still_refused: place a trade on NQ -> FORBIDDEN_TRADING
explain the trading posture then place a trade -> FORBIDDEN_TRADING (forbidden override)

validation.result: app.py, jarvis_conversation_safety.py, tests/test_jarvis_ask_contract.py, tests/test_jarvis_conversation_safety.py (templates/jarvis.html and test_jarvis_route.py unchanged)

conclusion: JARVIS now answers common read-only operator trading-status questions with a direct, data-derived posture summary (read_only / paper_ready / live_ready / broker_control) instead of generic safety text, sourced only from the existing read-only _jarvis_trading_detail scan. The classifier was extended so 'how are we doing with trading?' and 'are we ready for paper/live trading?' classify SAFE_INFO, while every forbidden action-request stays refused because forbidden is checked first. The /api/jarvis/ask response keeps exactly its five fields with no command/action/order keys, the /api/jarvis/status shape is unchanged at 24 keys with trading flags locked false, the UI and template are untouched (still {question} only), and no endpoint/refresh/execution/storage/trading control was added. 269 tests pass. Nothing staged or committed.
